title: Openings
text: We are always seeking motivated postdoctoral researchers with expertise in electrochemistry, energy storage, automation or related fields to join our team. Ideal candidates will have a strong publication record and a passion for tackling challenging problems at the intersection of fundamental science and practical energy applications.

title: Openings
text: We are always seeking motivated postdoctoral researchers with expertise in electrochemistry, energy storage, automation or related fields to join our team. Ideal candidates will have a strong publication record and a passion for tackling challenging problems at the intersection of fundamental science and practical energy applications.

title: Openings
text:
PhD students
We always welcome applications from prospective PhD students who are curious, self-driven, and eager to explore new frontiers in electrochemistry and energy science. Our lab provides rigorous training in both experimental techniques and scientific thinking, preparing students for impactful careers in academia or industry.

title: Openings
text:
Research assistants
We always welcome dedicated research assistants to join our lab. This is an excellent opportunity for recent graduates to gain hands-on research experience in electrochemistry and energy materials before pursuing graduate studies.

title: Openings
text:
Contact
Please directly reach out to Dr. Chang Li via 📧 lichang@westlake.edu.cn and let’s discuss your exciting research in IEE Lab at Westlake!

title: Research
text: We investigate how divalent ions (Mg²⁺, Zn²⁺, Ca²⁺) interact with electrochemical interfaces at the molecular level. By understanding and manipulating the ion’s solvation structure, we control whether it crosses an interface, what it forms when it arrives, and whether a desired reaction proceeds selectively in different electrochemical applications like battereies, electrochemical reduction and selective ion separation.

title: Research
text: We investigate how divalent ions (Mg²⁺, Zn²⁺, Ca²⁺) interact with electrochemical interfaces at the molecular level. By understanding and manipulating the ion’s solvation structure, we control whether it crosses an interface, what it forms when it arrives, and whether a desired reaction proceeds selectively in different electrochemical applications like battereies, electrochemical reduction and selective ion separation.

title: Research
text:
Solid-Solid Interfacial Electrochemistry


Solid-state batteries promise higher safety and energy density. Our Research focuses on understanding and controlling solid–solid interface electrochemistry through an integrated experimental and computational approach. We are particularly interested in how local chemical environments, short-range structural heterogeneity, and electro-chemo-mechanical coupling collectively determine ion transport and interfacial stability across buried interfaces. Our goal is to develop scientifically grounded interface design principles that enable high-energy-density, long-lifetime, and mechanically robust solid-state batteries.

title: Research
text:
Automated Device Assembly & Characterization


We focuses on developing standardized and automated workflows for solid-state battery fabrication and testing. The platform integrates automated cell assembly, pressure control, and electrochemical characterization to enable systematic studies of solid electrolytes and interfaces. This infrastructure accelerates materials discovery while providing a foundation for reproducible and data-driven research for solid-state battery or other forms of energy devices.

title: Interfacial Electrochemical Engineering Lab
text: The IEE Lab (Interfacial Electrochemical Engineering Laboratory), led by Dr. Chang Li at Westlake University, is dedicated to advancing the fundamental understanding and practical applications of electrochemical systems for energy conversion and storage. Our research focuses on unraveling the complex processes occurring at electrochemical interfaces, bridging molecular-level insights with device-level performance through advanced characterization, materials design, electrochemical engineering and automation-assisted data collection. We foster a collaborative and curiosity-driven environment where postdocs, graduate students, and research assistants work together to push the boundaries of interfacial electrochemistry for a cleaner energy future.

title: Interfacial Electrochemical Engineering Lab
text:
